ABOUT VACCINATIONS

Protecting Your Child From Day One

Vaccines are the most effective way to protect your child from serious diseases. We provide complete immunization services following the latest IAP guidelines.

Immunization is one of the most important things you can do to protect your child's health. Vaccines have eliminated or significantly reduced many dangerous childhood diseases that were once common.

At our clinic, we follow the Indian Academy of Pediatrics (IAP) immunization schedule, using only WHO-approved vaccines stored under strict cold chain conditions. We also offer painless vaccination options to minimize discomfort.

Our digital record system ensures you receive timely reminders for upcoming doses, and we provide vaccination certificates for school and travel requirements.

Why Vaccinate With Us

  • IAP-recommended schedule
  • Painless vaccination options
  • Cold chain maintained
  • Digital vaccination records
  • Reminder system for doses
  • Expert pediatric care

OUR SERVICES

Comprehensive Vaccination Services

From routine immunizations to travel vaccines, we provide complete vaccination care for children of all ages.

Routine Immunizations

Complete IAP-recommended vaccination schedule for infants, children, and adolescents from birth through 18 years.

Catch-Up Vaccination

Customized catch-up schedules for children who missed doses or are behind on their vaccination schedule.

Travel Vaccinations

Pre-travel immunizations including Hepatitis A & B, Typhoid, Yellow Fever, and other destination-specific vaccines.

Annual Flu Shots

Seasonal influenza vaccination for children 6 months and older to protect against flu complications.

HPV Vaccination

Human Papillomavirus vaccine for adolescents to prevent cervical cancer and other HPV-related diseases.

Painless Vaccinations

Combination vaccines and pain management techniques to minimize discomfort and needle anxiety.

IAP VACCINATION SCHEDULE

Recommended Immunization Timeline

Following the Indian Academy of Pediatrics guidelines for optimal protection.

01

Birth

BCG, Hepatitis B (1st dose), OPV Zero Dose

02

6 Weeks

DTaP, IPV, Hib, Hepatitis B (2nd), PCV, Rotavirus

03

10 Weeks

DTaP, IPV, Hib, PCV, Rotavirus

04

14 Weeks

DTaP, IPV, Hib, PCV, Rotavirus

05

6 Months

Hepatitis B (3rd dose), Influenza (annual)

06

9 Months

MMR (1st dose), Typhoid Conjugate

07

12 Months

Hepatitis A (1st dose), PCV Booster

08

15 Months

MMR (2nd dose), Varicella (1st dose)

DISEASES PREVENTED

Vaccines Protect Against

Immunization prevents these serious and potentially life-threatening diseases.

Diphtheria
Tetanus
Pertussis (Whooping Cough)
Polio
Hepatitis A & B
Measles
Mumps
Rubella
Chickenpox
Rotavirus
Pneumococcal Disease
Haemophilus Influenzae
Typhoid
Influenza (Flu)
Human Papillomavirus (HPV)
Meningococcal Disease

Don't Delay Your Child's Vaccinations

Catch-up schedules available. Book today to protect your child.

Book Now Call Now

FAQ

Vaccination Questions

Is the vaccination schedule safe?

Yes, we strictly follow the IAP (Indian Academy of Pediatrics) schedule which is scientifically proven safe and effective. Vaccines undergo rigorous testing before approval.

What if my child misses a vaccine dose?

Don't worry! Contact us immediately and we'll create a personalized catch-up schedule for your child. It's never too late to get back on track.

Are there any side effects?

Most side effects are mild and temporary - slight fever, soreness at injection site, or mild fussiness. Serious reactions are extremely rare. We monitor children after vaccination.

Can my child get vaccinated if they have a cold?

Mild illnesses usually don't prevent vaccination. However, if your child has a high fever or severe illness, we may postpone. Our doctor will assess each case.

Which optional vaccines do you recommend?

We recommend Influenza (annual), Varicella, Hepatitis A, Typhoid Conjugate, and HPV for adolescents. We discuss the benefits of each based on your child's needs.

Do you maintain cold chain for vaccines?

Absolutely! We maintain strict cold chain protocols with temperature-monitored refrigerators. All vaccines are stored and handled according to manufacturer guidelines.
